tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: You fly out today — finding stores now means you can hit them early in the trip.
tasks.reward: Gets glasses shopping mapped before you land, so you can act fast when jet lag is low.
nuggets: Kyoto has major optical chains near stations
Jins and Zoff are fast same-day options
Gion district has boutique eyewear shops
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto. I'm flying out today (March 23) for a 2-week Japan trip and want to buy 4 pairs of glasses early in the trip while I'm fresh. I need stores that can do prescriptions quickly (ideally same-day or next-day), accept US prescriptions, and are located near central Kyoto (staying near Gion/downtown). Give me a list of 5-7 stores with addresses, Google Maps links, estimated price ranges, and notes on turnaround time. Include both chain stores (Jins, Zoff) and any recommended boutiques.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent: subject 'Japan glasses coverage question'
tasks.hook_line: 4 pairs of glasses in Japan could save $400+ vs US prices if insurance allows it.
tasks.reward: Confirms whether you can expense glasses abroad — could make this trip pay for itself.
nuggets: US insurance sometimes covers foreign optical purchases
Need receipts in English for reimbursement
Prescription valid across borders with proper format
tasks.system_prompt: Help me draft an email to my insurance provider asking about coverage for buying eyeglasses in Japan. I have vision insurance (include typical US vision plan details). I want to buy 4 pairs of glasses during my March 23 - April 7 trip to save money compared to US prices. Questions to ask: (1) Will they reimburse foreign optical purchases? (2) What documentation do I need (receipts, prescriptions)? (3) Is there a limit on pairs per year? (4) Do I need pre-authorization? Keep the email concise and professional.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples best'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left after this.
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Also suggest 2-3 restaurants per day (breakfast, lunch, dinner) that are walkable from the activities. Note any early-morning opportunities to beat crowds.

tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the mountain town nobody talks about — hidden trails and countryside vibes.
tasks.reward: Locks in the Takayama leg so you can focus on Tokyo planning next.
nuggets: Hida beef is the local specialty
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 1.5 days in Takayama (March 27-28, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We want a mix of walking around the old town, outdoor activities (light hiking or nature walks), and good food. We're staying in central Takayama. Suggest: (1) must-see spots in the old town with walking routes, (2) 1-2 day trip options (Shirakawa-go? hiking trails?), and (3) where to eat Hida beef. Include opening hours, Google Maps links, and logistics for day trips (buses, times, costs).

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Tokyo neighborhoods where to stay guide'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is the final boss — plan this and the whole trip is locked.
tasks.reward: Completes all activity planning so you can just execute during the trip.
nuggets: Tokyo has 23 wards, each a different vibe
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 4-5 days in Tokyo (March 29 - April 2, 2026). Traveling with Dana. Interests: modern art, interesting neighborhoods, food, stationery/fountain pen shops, and a balance of busy and chill. We're staying in central Tokyo (not decided exactly where yet — suggest best area). Create a day-by-day guide with: (1) neighborhood focus per day (Shibuya, Shinjuku, Asakusa, etc.), (2) 2-3 activities per day with Google Maps links, (3) restaurant suggestions (including at least one high-end sushi spot), and (4) fountain pen/stationery shops to visit (Sailor, Pilot flagship stores). Include opening hours and transit notes.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open in VS Code
tasks.hook_line: Can you get a dev portal running without docs or handoff?
tasks.reward: Unblocks the entire Cerebro project — everything else waits on this.
nuggets: Developer portals usually need Docker + DB
Internal tooling often has config mysteries
First run always uncovers missing dependencies
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally. I'm picking this up cold with no handoff. It's a developer portal for engineers, built at my company. I need to: (1) identify what the tech stack is (likely Node/React + backend), (2) figure out environment setup (Docker? database? config files?), (3) troubleshoot first-run issues, and (4) get it serving on localhost. Walk me through a systematic approach to reverse-engineering the setup from the repo, including where to look for docs, config files, and common gotchas with internal tooling.

recommended_slots.reason: You fly out today — this is a 15-minute research task that sets you up for early trip success, and ramp_up energy is perfect for quick research wins.
Kyoto planning is the most engaging medium-effort task available, and with 2 days until arrival, this is the perfect time to lock it in before you're in transit.
Quick 10-minute email errand that could unlock $400+ in savings — low context cost makes it a perfect palette cleanser.
